steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence has two independent clauses: (1) 'the sculptures...are meant to challenge viewers to consider their own relationships to material ___' and (2) 'her thought provoking works...were no different.' The blank must end clause 1 and allow clause 2 to stand on its own. The word 'wastes' is needed to complete the prepositional phrase 'to material wastes.'

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘wastes;’: A semicolon correctly separates two independent clauses while 'wastes' completes the prepositional phrase. Both clauses are grammatically complete. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘wastes and to’: This creates a parallel structure ('to challenge...and to...') but leaves clause 2 without a subject, making it a fragment rather than an independent clause. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘wastes,’: A single comma between two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘wastes’: No punctuation between two independent clauses creates a run-on sentence. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
